REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS PCS, LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 5 DAYS TO ANSWER. ALLEGATIONS IN THE COMPLAINT MUST BE TAKEN AS TRUE FOR THE PURPOSES OF THE DEMURRER. DEFENDANT RAISED A FACTUAL DISPUTE REGARDING THE PROPRIETY OF TERMINATION, WHICH CANNOT BE ADDRESSED AT THIS STAGE OF THE LITIGATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY SHIFTED ITS BURDEN AND OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT WITH COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. THE COURT AGREES WITH THE PLAINTIFF THAT THE CONTRACT IS UNAMBIGUOUS, AND NO PAROL EVIDENCE IS CONSIDERED FOR THE CONTRACT INTERPRETATION. JUDGE: HARRY L. JACOBS ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Clifford Hicks 925-935-9800 of Hirsh Closson, APPLC appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
Raymond Paul Bolanos 415-268-9491 of AT & T Legal appearing for defendant.

Plaintiff contests tentative. Hearing held. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FELISSA CAGAN, TRUSTEE OF HIRSCH FAMILY TRUST MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Against Defendant New Cingular Wireless Pcs, Llc

is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show any breach of the operative lease agreement.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Clifford Hicks 925-935-9800 of Hirsh Closson, APPLC appearing via Zoom for plaintiff.
Raymond Paul Bolanos 415-268-9491 of AT & T Legal appearing for defendant.

Plaintiff contests tentative. Hearing held. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS PCS, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

is GRANTED. Moving party shifted its burden and opposing party failed to create a triable issue of fact with competent admissible evidence. The Court agrees with the Plaintiff that the contract is unambiguous, and no parol evidence is considered for the contract interpretation.

Judge: Harry L. Jacobs ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
